pi_items.source: PHP DateTimeZone standard (confirmed in https://www.php.net/manual/en/timezones.europe.php); Matomo uses PHP timezone identifiers for SitesManager_DefaultTimezone database option

ISO 4217 currency code standard (https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html); Matomo CurrencyDataProvider returns currency codes like EUR in array format; Matomo Marketplace documentation confirms EUR support

Matomo FAQ (https://matomo.org/faq/how-to/faq_72/) explicitly states users can select 'Yesterday' as default report date option; valid options are 'Today', 'Yesterday', 'Current week', 'Current month', 'Last 7 days', 'Last 30 days'

Matomo Developer Documentation UserPreferences API (https://developer.matomo.org/api-reference/Piwik/Plugins/UsersManager/UserPreferences) confirms valid period values are: 'day', 'week', 'month', 'year', and 'range'

privileged_info_summary: All four metadata configuration targets are verified as valid and correct. Expected timezone 'Europe/Paris' is a standard PHP DateTimeZone identifier used by Matomo for the SitesManager_DefaultTimezone database option. Expected currency 'EUR' is the ISO 4217 code for Euro, confirmed supported by Matomo's CurrencyDataProvider and Marketplace. Expected report date 'yesterday' is an officially documented valid option for Matomo user preferences. Expected report period 'week' is one of five valid period types ('day', 'week', 'month', 'year', 'range') in Matomo's reporting system. The task uses a real Matomo installation deployed via Docker with a test database.
